description: PRD-0: Foundation — Auth, Onboarding & Entity Setup for TaxEase Nigeria

PRD-0.name: Foundation — Auth, Onboarding & Entity Setup
PRD-0.overview: Establishes the foundational layer for TaxEase Nigeria — auth flow, onboarding wizard, app shell with side drawer navigation, profile management, entity management, and preferences. Without this, no other PRD can function.
PRD-0.background: TaxEase Nigeria is a tax compliance app for Nigerian freelancers and SMEs. PRD-0 is the root dependency — it delivers Clerk-based authentication, a 4-step onboarding wizard (user type, info, NIN/TIN, connect accounts), multi-entity support, and the empty app shell. The existing codebase has basic Clerk auth, a users/profiles/transactions schema, and simple pages — but needs a complete overhaul to match this PRD.
goals: Complete auth flow: email+password, Google OAuth, password reset via Clerk
4-step onboarding wizard that creates user profile and first tax entity
Multi-entity support with CRUD, switching, and soft-delete
App shell with side drawer navigation and entity selector
Profile management with NIN masking and avatar upload
User preferences for notifications and reminders
Category seed data for transaction classification
Logout and account deletion with data cleanup

coreConcepts.Entity: A tax entity (individual, business_name, or llc) for which a tax return is filed. Users can have multiple entities and switch between them.
coreConcepts.Onboarding: 4-step wizard: (1) User type, (2) Personal/business info + first entity creation, (3) NIN/TIN, (4) Connect accounts or skip. Sets onboardingComplete=true on finish.
coreConcepts.NIN: National Identification Number — 11 digits, serves as Tax ID under NTA 2025. Encrypted at rest.
coreConcepts.Category: Transaction category (income, business_expense, personal_expense, transfer). System categories are seeded; users can create custom ones later.

userStories.title: Overhaul Convex schema to match PRD-0 data models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema to support all PRD-0 features including expanded users, entities, categories, userPreferences, and connectedAccounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `users` table: add phone, nin, firsTin, userType ('freelancer'|'sme'), profession, preferredCurrency, onboardingComplete (boolean), avatarStorageId fields
Remove `profiles` table (merge its fields into `users`)
Add `entities` table with fields: userId, name, type ('individual'|'business_name'|'llc'), tin, rcNumber, vatRegistered, vatThresholdExceeded, isDefault, taxYearStart
Add `categories` table with fields: name, type ('income'|'business_expense'|'personal_expense'|'transfer'), isDeductibleDefault, ntaReference, isSystem, userId, icon, color
Add `userPreferences` table with fields: userId, deadlineReminderDays, vatReminderEnabled, uncategorisedAlertFrequency, invoiceOverdueDays, pushEnabled
Add `connectedAccounts` table with fields: entityId, userId, provider, providerAccountId, accountName, currency, accessToken, refreshToken, tokenExpiresAt, lastSyncedAt, status, errorMessage

Add indexes: users.by_clerk_user_id, users.by_email; entities.by_userId; categories.by_type, categories.by_userId; userPreferences.by_userId; connectedAccounts.by_userId, connectedAccounts.by_entityId
Keep existing `transactions` and `documents` tables unchanged
Update packages/shared types and Zod schemas to match new schema
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: See '§2 Entities' for TypeScript interfaces and '§7 Data Models' for table/index specs

userStories.title: Clerk webhook handler and auth helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Clerk webhooks to sync user data to Convex and robust auth helpers for all backend functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Clerk webhook HTTP action at POST /clerk-webhook that handles user.created, user.updated, user.deleted events
Install and use `svix` package to verify webhook signatures
On user.created: create `users` document with clerkUserId, email, fullName from Clerk payload, onboardingComplete=false
On user.updated: update corresponding `users` document fields (email, fullName)
On user.deleted: delete user and all associated data (entities, preferences, connected accounts)
Update convex/auth.ts: getCurrentUser should query by Clerk identity subject, getOrCreateCurrentUser should use webhook-created user (not auto-create)
Register webhook endpoint in convex/http.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: See '§6.1 Auth' for webhook spec. Clerk webhook must be registered in Clerk dashboard pointing to Convex HTTP endpoint.

userStories.title: User, entity, and preferences backend CRUD operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all backend mutations and queries for user profile, entities, preferences, and file uploads so the frontend can manage these resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Create users mutations: updateProfile (name, phone, profession, currency, avatar), updateNin (validate 11 digits, store), completeOnboarding (set onboardingComplete=true), uploadAvatar (accept storageId)
Create users queries: getMe (current user profile), getPreferences (userPreferences for current user)
Create users mutations: updatePreferences (all preference fields), createDefaultPreferences (called during onboarding)
Create entities mutations: create, update, setDefault (clear isDefault on others first), delete (soft-delete/archive)
Create entities queries: list (all for current user, exclude archived), get (by ID with ownership check)
Create files mutation: generateUploadUrl (return Convex Storage upload URL)
All mutations validate ownership (user can only modify their own data)
Create users.deleteAccount action: delete all user data in Convex + call Clerk Backend API to delete Clerk user
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: See '§6.2 Users', '§6.3 Entities', '§6.6 Files' for function specs

userStories.title: Categories seed data and dashboard query stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need system categories seeded and dashboard query stubs so the app has classification data and the dashboard can render empty states.
acceptanceCriteria: Create categories.seed mutation that inserts all system categories with isSystem=true
Seed income categories: Freelance/Client Income, Foreign Income, Investment Returns, Rental Income

Seed business expense categories (deductible): Internet & Data, Electricity & Fuel, Software Subscriptions, Equipment Purchase, Professional Development, Workspace/Rent, Transport (Business), Marketing & Advertising, Bank Charges
Seed personal expense categories: Personal — Groceries, Personal — Entertainment
Seed transfer categories: Transfer (Own Account), Loan Disbursement, Refund/Reimbursement
Create categories.listAll query returning system + user-created categories
Create dashboard.getSummary query stub that returns empty/placeholder summary for an entity
Seed mutation is idempotent (safe to run multiple times without duplicates)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: See '§7.2 Categories Seed Data' for full category list and '§6.7 Dashboard' for stub queries

userStories.title: Design system foundation with custom theme
userStories.description: As a user, I need a distinctive, polished interface that reflects the TaxEase brand rather than stock defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Install shadcn/ui and configure with custom theme (not defaults)
Configure custom Google Fonts in the app: display font (e.g., Outfit or Sora) for headings, body font (e.g., DM Sans or Plus Jakarta Sans) for text
Create custom color palette in globals.css matching PRD design tokens: primary #1A7F5E, primary-light #E8F5F0, accent #2B6CB0, success #38A169, warning #D69E2E, danger #E53E3E
Define neutral palette: neutral-900 #1A202C, neutral-500 #718096, neutral-100 #F7FAFC, white #FFFFFF
Add custom shadows (shadow-soft, shadow-medium) and animations (fade-in, slide-up) to tailwind.config
Configure typography scale: heading-xl 28px Bold, heading-lg 22px SemiBold, heading-md 18px SemiBold, body 15px Regular, body-sm 13px Regular, label 12px Medium
Body element has antialiased class applied
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: See '§4.1 Design Tokens' and '§4.2 Typography' for exact values. DESIGN: Visual foundation for all UI work.

userStories.title: Welcome screen and styled auth pages
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want a compelling welcome screen and polished sign-up/sign-in pages so I understand the app's value and can register easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace Landing.tsx with Welcome screen: hero illustration area, headline 'Tax compliance, made simple for Nigerians', subline 'Track income, file returns, and stay penalty-free — all in one place.'
Primary CTA 'Get Started' navigates to /sign-up, secondary link 'I already have an account' navigates to /sign-in
Style sign-up page: single-column form with TaxEase logo, full name, email, password with show/hide toggle, confirm password, 'Sign up with Google' button, Terms/Privacy links footer
Style sign-in page: email, password with show/hide toggle, 'Forgot password?' link, 'Log in with Google' button
Add routing logic: authenticated + onboarding complete → /app/dashboard, authenticated + onboarding incomplete → /app/onboarding, unauthenticated → / (welcome)
Password reset flow using Clerk's built-in reset (Forgot password → enter email → reset link → new password)
Duplicate email on sign-up shows 'Account already exists. Log in instead.' with link
All pages use new design system colors and typography
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-001/US-002/US-003/US-005/US-007 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Welcome, Sign Up, Log In layouts

userStories.title: Onboarding wizard — Steps 1 and 2
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to select my user type and enter my personal/business details so the app tailors my experience and creates my first tax entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/onboarding route protected by auth, accessible only when onboardingComplete=false
Step indicator showing 'Step X of 4' with progress bar across all onboarding steps
Step 1: Two selectable cards — Freelancer ('I earn income from clients, gigs, or self-employment') and SME ('I operate a registered business or company')
Step 1: Selection highlights card, enables 'Continue' button; selection saved to users.userType
Step 2 (Freelancer): First name, Last name (pre-filled from sign-up), Primary profession dropdown, Primary income currency (NGN/USD/GBP/EUR)
Step 2 (SME): Business name, Business type (Registered Business Name / LLC), Industry dropdown, Annual turnover range dropdown
Step 2 saves data to users table and creates first entity: Freelancer → individual entity with user's name; SME → business_name or llc entity with business name
First entity marked isDefault=true with taxYearStart=1 (January)
Back button on Step 2 returns to Step 1; Back disabled on Step 1
Form validation with inline error messages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-008/US-009 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Onboarding layout with step indicator

userStories.title: Onboarding wizard — Steps 3 and 4, complete onboarding
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to enter my NIN/TIN and optionally connect accounts, then land on a ready-to-use dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 3: Explanatory text about NIN under NTA 2025, NIN input (11 digits), optional FIRS TIN field
Step 3: Info callout 'Your NIN is stored securely and never shared without your consent'
Step 3: NIN validated as exactly 11 numeric digits with inline error on invalid input
Step 3: NIN stored in users.nin, FIRS TIN stored in users.firsTin
Step 4: List of import option cards — Upload bank statement, Connect bank account, Connect Paystack/Flutterwave, Connect Payoneer/Wise, 'I'll do this later'
Step 4: All options are placeholders for PRD-0; 'I'll do this later' always available and enables 'Finish Setup'
Tapping 'Finish Setup' calls completeOnboarding mutation (sets onboardingComplete=true) and creates default userPreferences
User navigates to /app/dashboard with success toast 'You're all set! Add transactions when you're ready.'
Back buttons work correctly across Steps 3 and 4
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-010/US-011 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Onboarding layout. Step 4 connections are deferred to PRD-1/PRD-8.

userStories.title: App shell with side drawer navigation and entity selector
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a side drawer to navigate between app sections and switch entities so I can access all features efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Redesign AppShell with proper side drawer: mobile = overlay from left, web = persistent collapsible sidebar
Drawer top: user avatar (or initials), full name, email
Entity selector dropdown visible when user has 2+ entities; hidden for single-entity users
Selecting a different entity updates global entity context and refreshes current screen data
Nav items: Dashboard, Transactions, Invoices, Tax Summary, Filing, Reports (Invoices/Tax Summary/Filing/Reports as placeholder routes)
Divider section: Settings, Help & Support, Documents
Bottom: Log Out button
Active nav item highlighted with primary-light (#E8F5F0) background
Tapping nav item navigates and closes drawer on mobile
Header has hamburger icon (mobile), app title, notification bell icon
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-013 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Side Drawer spec and '§4.4 Platform Behaviour' for mobile vs web drawer

userStories.title: Dashboard shell with empty states
userStories.description: As a user who completed onboarding, I want the dashboard to show a proper layout with empty states so I know where I am and what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard layout: header with title 'Dashboard', scrollable card sections
Tax Position Summary card showing '2026 Tax Year' with 'No transactions yet' placeholder
Quick Stats row with zero/placeholder values for income, expenses, tax estimate
Recent Transactions section with empty state: illustration + 'No transactions yet. Import a bank statement to get started.' + 'Import Now' CTA
CTAs navigate to placeholder routes or existing Transactions page
Dashboard uses entity context (shows data for currently selected entity)
All sections use consistent empty state pattern (illustration + headline + CTA) from design system
Header consistent with new design system
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-012 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Dashboard layout and '§4.5 Global Components' for empty state pattern

userStories.title: Profile view and edit with avatar upload
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to view and edit my profile so my information stays accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile view at Settings → Profile showing: avatar, full name, email, phone, NIN (masked as •••••••1234), FIRS TIN, primary currency
Avatar shows uploaded photo or initials fallback
'Edit' button opens edit form with all editable fields
Avatar upload: file picker uploads to Convex Storage, updates avatarStorageId
NIN edit re-validates 11 digits before saving
All fields save via updateProfile/updateNin mutations with inline validation errors
Success toast on save
Danger zone section at bottom with 'Delete Account' link (navigates to delete flow)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-014/US-015 — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Profile layout

userStories.title: Entity management — list, create, edit, delete, and switch
userStories.description: As a user with one or more tax entities, I want to manage them from Settings so I can add new businesses, update details, and remove obsolete ones.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings → Tax Entities shows list of entity cards (name, type, tax year, default badge)
'Add Entity' button opens create form: Name, Type (individual/business_name/llc), TIN, RC Number, VAT registered toggle, VAT threshold exceeded toggle
Tapping entity card opens detail view with all entity fields and 'Edit', 'Set as Default', 'Delete' actions
Edit form saves via entities.update mutation with type-specific validation (RC Number required for LLC)
'Set as Default' updates isDefault, clears it from other entities
Delete shows confirmation dialog: 'Delete {name}? All transactions and invoices will be preserved but no longer editable.' with soft-delete
Cannot delete last remaining entity (button disabled or error)
If deleted entity was default, another entity auto-becomes default
New entities appear in drawer entity selector; deleted entities disappear
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-016/US-017/US-018/US-019/US-019a — See '§4.3 Screen Layouts' for Tax Entities layout

userStories.title: Preferences, logout, and delete account
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage my notification preferences, log out securely, and delete my account if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings → Notifications shows preference form: filing deadline reminders (toggle + lead days 30/14/7/1), VAT return reminders (toggle), uncategorised alert frequency (daily/weekly/off), invoice overdue days (1/3/7), push notifications toggle
Preferences save via updatePreferences mutation with success toast
Default preferences created during onboarding (or on first access if missing)
Log Out in side drawer shows confirmation dialog 'Are you sure you want to log out?'
On confirm: Clerk.signOut() called, session cleared, navigate to Welcome screen
Delete Account (from Profile danger zone) shows confirmation dialog with explicit warning about permanent data deletion
Delete Account requires password entry (or re-authentication) to confirm
On confirm: deleteAccount action removes all user data from Convex + deletes Clerk user, navigates to Welcome
User cannot access protected routes after logout or deletion
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: US-020/US-021/US-022 — See '§4.5 Global Components' for confirmation dialog pattern

userStories.title: Polish pass — animations, loading states, and empty states
userStories.description: As a user, I need micro-interactions, loading states, and consistent empty states for a polished experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Page content uses fade-in animation on load
Navigation items have hover transforms and smooth transitions
Active states (nav, entity selector, onboarding cards) have smooth transitions
Loading states use skeleton placeholders (not spinners) for all data-dependent screens
Toast notifications properly positioned and styled: Success (green), Warning (amber), Error (red), Info (blue)
Confirmation dialogs follow pattern: title, consequence description, Cancel, Confirm (destructive actions use red)
All empty states follow consistent pattern: illustration area + headline + CTA
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD-0: DESIGN — See '§4.5 Global Components' for toast, empty state, and dialog patterns. Polish pass after core functionality.
